name: Pinworms (Enterobiasis)
ontario_ma_scope: ⚠️ Pinworms/enterobiasis is NOT a designated Ontario MA condition. Pyrantel pamoate (Combantrin) is OTC — pharmacist provides OTC counselling only.

key_questions: Perianal itch (especially nocturnal — hallmark: female worms lay eggs at night)?
Child in daycare/school (highly transmissible in children)?
In practice, empirical treatment is standard in Canada when classic symptoms (nocturnal perianal itch, especially in children) are present — confirm diagnosis not always required.
Scotch tape (pinworm paddle) test: apply sticky tape to perianal area in morning before bathing, examine microscopically for eggs — highest sensitivity when done before bathing or defecation
red_flags: Significant weight loss, diarrhea, or malabsorption → other parasitic infection
Pelvic symptoms in girls → pinworm migration to vagina/uterus (requires gynaecology)

first_line.name: Pyrantel Pamoate (Practical First-Line in Canada)
first_line.drugs: Pyrantel pamoate (Combantrin) 11 mg/kg single dose (max 1g) — repeat in 2 weeks
first_line.notes: PYRANTEL PAMOATE (Combantrin) IS THE PRACTICAL FIRST-LINE IN CANADA. Mebendazole (Vermox) was discontinued in Canada ~2012 and has had intermittent, unreliable availability since — as of 2024–2025, NOT consistently available in Canadian pharmacies. Single dose is effective — repeat in 2 weeks to kill worms hatched from eggs after first dose. TREAT THE ENTIRE HOUSEHOLD simultaneously (re-infection cycle). Pyrantel pamoate is OTC in Canada. Safe in children ≥2 years per Combantrin Canadian monograph (some references cite ≥1 year — note this discrepancy). Pyrantel pamoate poorly absorbed — preferred if treatment during pregnancy deemed necessary. Pregnancy: mebendazole and albendazole generally avoided in first trimester — consult physician.
first_line.preferred_when: All household contacts treated simultaneously; first-line practical choice in Canada

second_line.name: Mebendazole (when available)
second_line.drugs: Mebendazole 100 mg single dose — repeat in 2 weeks
second_line.notes: As of 2024–2025, mebendazole (Vermox) is NOT consistently available in Canadian pharmacies. Use pyrantel pamoate as practical alternative. Requires prescription in Canada.

second_line.name: Albendazole (when mebendazole unavailable)
second_line.drugs: Albendazole 400 mg single dose — repeat in 2 weeks
second_line.notes: Available via Special Access Programme (SAP) or compounding in Canada when mebendazole is unavailable. Generally avoided in first trimester of pregnancy.

non_pharm: Treat ALL household members simultaneously — key to eradication
Wash all bedding, towels, underwear, and clothing in hot water on day of treatment; avoid shaking bedding (disperses eggs)
Trim fingernails short (eggs under nails are main transmission route); discourage nail-biting and thumb-sucking
Strict hand-washing especially after toilet use and before meals
Morning shower — removes eggs deposited overnight in perianal area
refer_when: Ectopic infection (vaginal, peritoneal — pelvic symptoms in girls)
Uncertainty about diagnosis or atypical presentation

references: Ontario College of Pharmacists. Minor Ailments: Pinworm Infection (Enterobiasis). OCP Prescribing Standards, O. Reg. 256/24 (2025).
Canadian Paediatric Society (CPS). Common questions about Enterobius vermicularis (pinworm) management (2018, reaffirmed 2023).
AAP (American Academy of Pediatrics). Red Book: Report of the Committee on Infectious Diseases, 32nd ed. Pinworm Infection (Enterobiasis). 2021.
WHO. Soil-transmitted helminthiases: Enterobiasis (Pinworm). World Health Organization (2023).
